The boxer is known for his power.
His style is very aggressive.
He fought a professional opponent.
Their first match was intense.
He landed one hundred twenty punches.
That number was in the first fight.
The second match followed soon.
He improved his punch count.
He increased by twenty five percent.
We must calculate that number.
One hundred twenty times point two five is thirty.
So he landed fifteen punches more.
That makes one hundred fifty total.
The third match was next.
He landed thirty more punches.
One hundred fifty plus thirty is one eighty.
That was his third match count.
Now we need the total sum.
Add one hundred twenty to one hundred fifty.
That equals two hundred seventy.
Now add one hundred eighty to that.
Two hundred seventy plus one hundred eighty.
The sum is four hundred fifty.
Four hundred fifty punches total.
He was very powerful indeed.
His aggression was on display.
The crowd was very loud.
The referee watched closely.
Each punch counted for the score.
The series was highly anticipated.
Fans loved the action.
The math was simple to solve.
The answer is four hundred fifty.
We have solved the problem.
The boxer won the day.
